---
title: "modelling clutch size"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(performance)
library(MASS)
library(pscl)
library(dplyr)
library(glmmTMB)
```

AMBIENT, WARM AND HOT LARVAL TEMPERATURE

```{r}
results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#remove ambient, because these need to be modelled separately. also remove larval treatment of cool and mean, because the sample size isn't big enough to model - just not enough clutches.
#I think I will model the ambient larva treatment separately.
results_filtered <- results %>%
  filter(
    larval_treatment %in% c("ambient", "warm", "hot")) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(weight_mg, egg_mean_1A, clutch_size),
    ~ as.numeric(scale(.)),
    .names = "{.col}_z"
  ))


results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = factor(larval_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "warm","hot")),
    pupal_treatment = factor(pupal_treatment, levels = c("ambient", "cool","mean","warm","hot"))
  )


```

```{r}
#look at the distribution of clutch size
ggplot(results, aes(x = clutch_size)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5) +  # change this value to control bin width +
  labs(title = "Distribution of clutch size",
       x = "clutch size",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()


#mean and variance of clutch size
# mean
mean(results_filtered$clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE)

# variance
var(results_filtered$clutch_size, na.rm = TRUE)

#the mean is much greater than the variance, suggesting that a negative binomial model might be better than the poisson model

#testing whether poisson or negative binomial model is better

pois_mod <- glm(clutch_size ~ 1, family = poisson(link = "log"), data = results)

nb_mod <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~ 1, data = results)

AIC(pois_mod, nb_mod)
#the AIC is way, way lower for the negative binomial model, so this is the better model to use.




```



```{r}
mod.hurdl_4 <- glmmTMB(clutch_size ~ larval_treatment + pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered + (1 | maternalID), ziformula = ~ larval_treatment + pupal_treatment + weight_mg_centered, family = truncated_nbinom2, data = results_filtered)
summary(mod.hurdl_4)
AIC(mod.hurdl_4)
#2467



```





```{r}
library(DHARMa)

# simulate residuals
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(mod.hurdl_4)

# basic diagnostic plots
plot(sim_res)

testDispersion(sim_res)

testZeroInflation(sim_res)


#plot observed vs predicted
pred <- predict(mod.hurdl_4, type = "response")

plot(pred, results_filtered$clutch_size)
abline(0, 1, col = "red")
```
